mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment signals an expansion in labor demand that boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in supply that boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days, but only when the unemployment rate is below 5%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the inflation rate is below 2%.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment signals an expansion in labor demand that boosts equity prices over the next 60 trading days, but only when the real GDP growth rate is above 2%.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in supply that boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days, but only when the federal funds rate is below 4%.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.05).
